criteria_checked.note: E1 says 'delegate the remaining argv to RCORE unchanged' but the current dispatch is `exec python3 "$RCORE" "$sub" "$@"` (bin/dmc:375). exec never returns; composition steps 2-5 (read pointer, compile, validate, ARMED line) AND the E2 WARNING are all post-delegation, so they are unreachable unless the `start` verb is branched OUT of the shared exec group into a captured call. The plan never states this restructuring — its load-bearing implementability step is left implicit.

ACs are concrete and fixture-driven. One gap: the back-compat AC pins exit code + stdout identity but not the REFUSE (exit-3) path, which tests/fixtures/m6/test-adversarial.sh:277 captures via `2>&1` and greps — bin/dmc must add nothing on non-zero RCORE exit (WARNING is exit-0-only). Add refuse-path byte-identity (both streams) to the back-compat criterion.
Defect root-cause, manual-procedure history, and envelope are accurate and verifiable in-tree.

All paths exist, BUT the literal compile invocation `python3 bin/lib/dmc-scope-lock.py ... --out .harness/runs/<id>/scope.lock.json` uses a cwd-relative tool path and cwd-relative artifact paths. bin/dmc has no scope-lock lib variable (only RCORE et al.); the executor must add `$HERE/lib/dmc-scope-lock.py` (absolute) — a relative `bin/lib/...` breaks whenever cwd != repo root, which is the norm (see blocker B2).

Assumption #1's fallback claim is false: dmc-run-lifecycle.py and dmc-scope-lock.py do NOT read CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR — isolation is `--root` (default '.'=cwd) only (grep confirms no CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR ref in either CLI). runs_dir(root)=<root>/.harness/runs (dmc-run-lifecycle.py:229), so pointer/lock are root-rooted, not cwd-rooted. The plan's cwd-relative composition contradicts this (blocker B2). Assumption #2 (no fixture asserts run-start stderr empty on success) VERIFIED MET — every success-path fixture uses `>/dev/null 2>&1`; run-core self-tests invoke the module directly (dmc-run-lifecycle.py:680 `subprocess.run([sys.executable, __file__, ...])`), never bin/dmc, so the dispatch flag cannot disturb them.

Tight: core modules byte-untouched, Out-of-Scope explicit, --scope-input kept optional (back-compat). bin/dmc==enforcement CONFIRMED (dmc-repo-intel.py:332 classify_landmark → FLAG expected); .claude/skills falls through classify_landmark → NOT enforcement/protected, so no G4 override needed — plan claim CONFIRMED. Manifest lists bin/dmc + both dmc-start-work/SKILL.md by path (INSTALL_MANIFEST.md:12,121,160) → modification-neutral; new tests/install file not enumerated — neutrality claims CONFIRMED.

Security lens (mandatory — enforcement-class bin/dmc): fail-closed teardown ordering is CORRECT — cmd_suspend resolves via pointer (dmc-run-lifecycle.py:432-433), so `run suspend` MUST precede pointer removal, which the plan has right. Net-positive: closes the false-armed gap. Residual low risk: if `RCORE suspend` itself fails mid-teardown the RUNNING+no-lock state persists — but RUNNING→SUSPENDED is always a valid transition, so low. No secret exposure; scope-input is compiler-validated. NOTE: the pre-existing M6 verdict-value floor (dmc-run-lifecycle.py:383-390) already refuses `start` on a plan-bound REJECT — independent of this change.

LOCAL-commit ceiling, staged-set==in-scope, clean-tree --all 802/3/3 with .codex/config.toml stashed (currently modified in tree — V15 gotcha handled). The teardown IS the in-flight rollback for a half-armed run.

blockers.statement: The `run start` dispatch is `exec python3 "$RCORE" "$sub" "$@"` (bin/dmc:375), shared across start|suspend|resume|status|block|blocked-status|unblock. exec replaces the process and never returns, so E1's post-delegation composition (read run-id, compile, validate, print ARMED) and E2's post-success WARNING are unreachable as the plan describes ('delegate the remaining argv to RCORE unchanged'). The plan must explicitly state that the `start` subcommand is split OUT of the shared exec group into a NON-exec captured call (`python3 "$RCORE" start <argv>; rc=$?`) — stdout left to flow through unmodified (preserving byte-identity), then branch on rc and --scope-input presence. All other run subcommands keep `exec`.

The composition must be --root-aware; the plan's literal recipe is cwd-relative and will misbuild against the established caller pattern. runs_dir(root)=<root>/.harness/runs (dmc-run-lifecycle.py:229), and every existing fixture invokes `"$REPO/bin/dmc" run start --plan P --root "$REPO"` WITHOUT cd-ing in (tests/fixtures/m9/test-e2e-loop.sh:99, m6/_m6common.sh:179, m7/_m7common.sh:149, m6.5/_m65common.sh:185), reading the run-id back from `<root>/.harness/runs/current-run-id`. The new E4 test will do the same. The plan reads the pointer from `.harness/runs/current-run-id` and compiles `--out .harness/runs/<id>/scope.lock.json` with NO `--root` (compiler defaults to cwd) and a relative `bin/lib/...` tool path — so when cwd != --root (the norm) it reads/writes the REAL repo's .harness/runs, breaking AC-1 and risking real-repo contamination. The plan must specify that bin/dmc extracts `--root` (default '.') AND `--plan`/`--scope-input` from the start argv and threads --root into: the pointer read (<root>/.harness/runs/current-run-id), the compile `--out <root>/.harness/runs/<id>/scope.lock.json`, and the compile+validate `--root <root>`; and uses `$HERE/lib/dmc-scope-lock.py` (absolute), not a relative bin/lib path.
